report_name: Unit 42 Enables Phish-Resistant MFA for RDP by Reverse-Engineering WebAuthn Protocol

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) is identified as a primary vector for lateral movement and initial access, which this research aims to secure by enabling FIDO2/WebAuthn phish-resistant MFA.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The research described in the article directly mitigates attacks targeting Valid Accounts, such as credential theft and password spraying, by enabling the use of phish-resistant MFA for RDP sessions.

tools.name: mstsc.exe
tools.description: The native Windows RDP client, which was analyzed to understand the MS-RDPEWA protocol. Monitoring its execution is suggested as a hunting hint.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor of the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) and the undocumented MS-RDPEWA virtual channel protocol that was reverse-engineered by Unit 42.

identities.name: Okta
identities.description: Mentioned as an example of a service that uses WebAuthn for authentication, which could be accessed within a secured RDP session.

identities.name: Yubico
identities.description: Vendor of the YubiKey, which is mentioned as an example of a phish-resistant hardware authenticator compatible with the described WebAuthn redirection solution.

indicators.name: Microsoft RDP Client Execution Monitoring
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Microsoft RDP Client Execution
id: 2b9c1f8a-3e4d-4f5a-8b6c-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Monitors for the execution of the native Windows RDP client (mstsc.exe). This can be used to baseline RDP usage and detect anomalous client execution.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/webauthn-added-to-browser-based-rdp/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1021.001
level: informational
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mstsc.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the native Windows RDP client, mstsc.exe. This can be used to baseline RDP usage as part of a broader monitoring strategy.

indicators.name: WebAuthn over RDP Virtual Channel Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: WebAuthn over RDP Virtual Channel Detection
id: 8a1b2c3d-4e5f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the WebAuthn Dynamic Virtual Channel (DVC) within an RDP session by looking for its specific name in logs. While benign in configured environments, its unexpected appearance could indicate policy bypass or anomalous activity.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/webauthn-added-to-browser-based-rdp/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1021.001
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'Microsoft::Windows::RDP.Webauthn'
  condition: keywords
indicators.description: Detects the use of the 'Microsoft::Windows::RDP.Webauthn' Dynamic Virtual Channel (DVC). While its presence is benign in a properly configured environment, an unexpected appearance or anomalous traffic patterns could warrant investigation.

relationships.source_name: WebAuthn over RDP Virtual Channel Detection
